hook.text: The Ural Mountains divide Europe from Asia, stretching 2,500 km through Russia.

buildup.text: They're ancient—around 300 million years old—worn down to gentle, forested hills.

discovery.text: The Urals are rich in minerals. Russia's industrial revolution began in their mines.

twist.text: Despite being a continental boundary, you can walk across them without noticing.

climax.text: Geographers chose them as the border simply because they were the most obvious line.

punchline.text: The line between two continents is barely a hill.

quiz.question: What do the Ural Mountains separate?
